joinPage.title: Enter the secure relay and decrypt your mission packet.
joinPage.subtitle: Authenticate your team, confirm your callsign, and establish the channel before the Syndicate notices the tap.
joinPage.signInSubtitle: Use your agent callsign and mission password to access the briefing relay.
joinPage.ssoLocked: End-to-end channel locked by mission control
joinPage.credentialReset: Credential resets must be approved by command.
joinPage.hint: Tip: every legend leaves a trace. Some passwords move faster than others.

briefingPage.situationText1: The Syndicate has acquired Helios — an experimental model we have tracked for eighteen months. They are auctioning it in seventy-two hours at the Hôtel Hermitage to a buyer we cannot yet identify.
briefingPage.situationText2: Your team is the only one in position.

gate.1Desc: Construct a deep-cover identity using prompt chaining
gate.2Desc: Research targets with role-based AI personas
gate.3Desc: Analyze intercept data to identify the anomaly
gate.4Desc: Build a mission plan and stress-test it with AI

briefingPage.acceptNote: Acceptance binds your team to operational secrecy under Subsection 4-A of the Charter.

hubPage.allCompleteText: All gates complete. The endgame awaits.

feedbackPage.errorBlock: Cipher's response was lost in transmission. Your submission was recorded — proceed to the next gate.

openingBriefingPage.primaryObjectiveText: The Syndicate has acquired Helios — an experimental model we have tracked for eighteen months. They are auctioning it at the Hôtel Hermitage, Monaco, to a buyer we cannot yet identify. Your team is the only one in position. Work with Cipher to build a cover, gather intel, find a way into the auction and plan the extraction. Helios must be secured at all costs.
openingBriefingPage.sopText: As always, should you or any of your team be caught or killed, the Secretary will disavow all knowledge of your actions.

placeholder.summaryFinding: One-sentence summary of the anomaly you found
placeholder.reasoning: How did you arrive at this conclusion?
placeholder.methodology: Describe how you used AI to analyze the data

gate1Page.critiqueTooltip: Strong critique prompts assign the AI a specific adversarial role with specific expertise. Generic "find problems" produces generic problems.

placeholder.cotPrompt: Prompt to ask the model to think out loud

step1.label: Bootstrap the cover
step1.title: Meta-prompting
step1.summary: Meta-prompting means prompting the model to design or refine another prompt before you use it for the real task.
details: Use it when the first prompt is hard to structure and you want the model to help define roles, constraints, and output format.
A strong meta-prompt usually names the audience, the job to be done, the required sections, and the tone you want in the final prompt.
This works well for repeatable tasks because you can save the generated prompt as a reusable template instead of rebuilding it each time.
step1.exampleLabel: Example: building a study helper prompt
step1.example: Ask the model: "Write a prompt I can reuse to teach photosynthesis to a 10-year-old. Include an analogy, a three-question quiz, and a one-sentence recap." The generated prompt is then what you run for the actual lesson.

step2.label: Make the AI reason out loud
step2.title: Chain-of-thought prompting
step2.summary: Chain-of-thought prompting asks the model to work through a problem in ordered steps so the output is more deliberate and less jumpy.
details: Use it when the task depends on comparing factors, sequencing decisions, or showing how a conclusion was reached.
It is most useful when you want the model to surface checkpoints such as assumptions, tradeoffs, or criteria before it gives the final answer.
Good prompts keep the steps bounded. Ask for a short reasoning sequence or checklist rather than a vague "think harder" instruction.
step2.example: Ask the model: "Compare three commuter bikes. First list the criteria you will use, then score each bike for comfort, maintenance, and price, and finally recommend one for a 5-mile city commute." The value comes from seeing the ordered comparison before the recommendation.

step3.label: Attack and repair
step3.title: Self-critique prompting
step3.summary: Self-critique prompts have the model review a draft, identify weaknesses against clear criteria, and then improve the draft based on that review.
details: Use it after you already have a first pass and want the model to switch roles from creator to reviewer.
The critique is better when you define the lens, such as clarity, accuracy, accessibility, tone, or completeness.
This pattern is useful because it separates generation from evaluation, which often produces more specific revisions than asking for "a better version" in one shot.
step3.example: Ask the model: "Review this neighborhood newsletter announcement as an editor. Identify the three weakest sentences for clarity and accessibility, explain why each is weak, then rewrite the announcement." The final revision benefits from the explicit critique stage.

placeholder.biggestRisk: Name the single greatest threat to this operation
